title: Pakistani Aloo Keema

subs.name: frozen green peas (matar keema variant)
subs.notes: add in the last 5 minutes, no extra water needed

steps.text: Heat oil in a heavy pan over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 15 seconds, then add the chopped onion. Fry, stirring often, for 7–8 minutes until the onion is a rich golden-brown.

Add ginger-garlic paste and stir for 1 minute until the raw smell disappears. Add the chopped tomatoes, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook for 5 minutes on medium heat, stirring and pressing the tomatoes, until they soften completely.

Add the mince and break it apart immediately with a spoon or spatula so no large lumps remain. Fry on high heat for 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mince loses its pink colour and the water it releases has evaporated.

Add the cubed potatoes and green chillies. Stir well to coat the potatoes in the masala, then pour in 150 ml water. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce to low heat, and cook for 15 minutes until the potatoes are completely tender when pierced.

Remove the lid and cook on medium heat for 3–4 minutes, stirring often, until most of the liquid has dried up and the mince and potato cling together in a thick, barely-moist mixture. Sprinkle garam masala and fold in.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with chapati, puri, or steamed rice. A dollop of butter on top adds an extra layer of richness.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: keema → 400 g chicken mince (reduce cooking time by 5 minutes); potato → frozen green peas (matar keema variant) (add in the last 5 minutes, no extra water needed); oil → 2 tbsp ghee (richer flavour)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This dish is almost identical to the north Indian aloo keema and requires no special adaptation. Cook exactly as written in any Indian karahi or pressure cooker. For a pressure cooker version, after adding the potatoes and water, cook for 2 whistles on medium heat, open naturally, and finish on high heat to dry the masala. Mutton mince is available at any halal butcher; chicken mince is sold at all major Indian supermarkets.
